decisions.rationale: The gap consists only of a comma, which is punctuation used to separate clauses and has no independent assertive meaning.
The word "while" is a non-assertive connector that links two clauses without adding independent semantic content.
The gap consists only of a comma, which is punctuation used to separate clauses and has no independent assertive meaning.
The gap consists only of a period, which is sentence-terminating punctuation and has no independent assertive meaning.
The word "When" is a non-assertive connector that introduces a conditional clause, and its semantic role is fully captured by the relationship between the existing claims.
The gap consists only of a comma, which is punctuation used to separate clauses and has no independent assertive meaning.
The gap consists only of a period, which is sentence-terminating punctuation and has no independent assertive meaning.
The gap consists only of a semicolon, which is punctuation used to connect related clauses and has no independent assertive meaning.
The gap consists only of a period, which is sentence-terminating punctuation and has no independent assertive meaning.
The word "If" is a non-assertive connector that introduces a conditional clause, and its semantic role is fully captured by the relationship between the existing claims.
The word "or" serves as a list delimiter connecting two alternative conditions, and its function is fully represented by the relationship between the two adjacent claims.

The gap contains the assertive clause "the pattern is falsified," which represents the consequence of the conditions described in the preceding claims and appears to be claim-bearing content omitted from the current boundary.
The word "or" serves as a list delimiter connecting two alternative observables, and its function is fully represented by the relationship between the two adjacent claims.
The gap consists only of a period, which is sentence-terminating punctuation and has no independent assertive meaning.
